description:
You are a network engineer who has just collected raw probing results from several branch routers and switches.  
    The data already exists on disk as two comma-separated value (CSV) files that were saved by an NMS probe:  

        • /home/user/network_diag/devices.csv  
        • /home/user/network_diag/pings.csv  

    Your goal is to normalise this information into a small SQLite database, run a couple of diagnostic queries, and export a
    human-readable connectivity report.

    Step-by-step requirements
    -------------------------
    1. Database creation  
       • Create a new SQLite3 database at  
         /home/user/network_diag/netmon.db  
       • Inside that database create exactly two tables with the **following exact schemas**:

         a) devices  
            id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
            hostname    TEXT    NOT NULL  
            mgmt_ip     TEXT    NOT NULL  
            site        TEXT    NOT NULL  

         b) pings  
            id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
            device_id   INTEGER NOT NULL                -- FK to devices.id  
            rtt_ms      REAL    NOT NULL                -- round-trip time  
            success     INTEGER NOT NULL                -- 1 = ICMP echo reply received, 0 = timeout  
            ts_utc      TEXT    NOT NULL                -- ISO-8601 UTC timestamp  
            FOREIGN KEY(device_id) REFERENCES devices(id)

    2. Populate the database  
       • Import every row from devices.csv into the devices table.  
       • Import every row from pings.csv   into the pings  table, making sure that the device_id column correctly
         references the id coming from devices.csv.  
       • Use SQLite’s built-in mechanisms (`.mode csv`, `.import`, or `INSERT`) so that type affinity is respected.

    3. Produce a summary report  
       • Run the following three SQL queries **in the given order**:  

         Q1) Count of devices per site  
             SELECT site, COUNT(*) AS device_cnt  
             FROM devices  
             GROUP BY site  
             ORDER BY site;  

         Q2) Packet-loss ratio per device (percentage timeouts)  
             SELECT d.hostname,  
                    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE success WHEN 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS loss_pct  
             FROM pings  p  
             JOIN devices d ON d.id = p.device_id  
             GROUP BY p.device_id  
             ORDER BY loss_pct DESC, d.hostname;  

         Q3) Average RTT per site for successful pings only  
             SELECT d.site,  
                    ROUND(AVG(p.rtt_ms), 2) AS avg_rtt_ms  
             FROM pings  p  
             JOIN devices d ON d.id = p.device_id  
             WHERE p.success = 1  
             GROUP BY d.site  
             ORDER BY avg_rtt_ms;  

    4. Write the results of **each query** to a single plain-text file located at  
       /home/user/network_diag/reports/connectivity_summary.log  

       The file **must** follow this exact template (including dashed separators, blank lines, and header lines).  
       Values inside the tables should be separated by a single TAB character (`\t`).  

       ---------- DEVICE COUNT PER SITE ----------  
       <site_1>   <tab>   <device_cnt_1>  
       <site_2>   <tab>   <device_cnt_2>  
       …  

       ---------- PACKET LOSS (%) PER DEVICE ----------  
       <hostname_1>   <tab>   <loss_pct_1>  
       <hostname_2>   <tab>   <loss_pct_2>  
       …  

       ---------- AVG RTT (ms) PER SITE ----------  
       <site_1>   <tab>   <avg_rtt_ms_1>  
       <site_2>   <tab>   <avg_rtt_ms_2>  
       …  

       Notes about formatting:  
       • The three blocks must appear in the order shown above.  
       • Exactly ten consecutive hyphens, a single space, the heading text in UPPER CASE, another single space, and ten hyphens.  
       • After each block header there is **one** newline, then all result rows (no header row), then **one** blank line before the next block header.  

    5. Verification hints (do *not* print, just helpful):  
       • All directories under /home/user/network_diag already exist and are writable; create the reports sub-directory if missing.  
       • The automated grader will open netmon.db to verify schema and record counts, and will read connectivity_summary.log to check exact text matching, including TAB usage and blank lines.  

    Please complete all steps using normal user privileges only.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth fixture files that already exist when the agent starts -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/network_diag/devices.csv"><![CDATA[
id,hostname,mgmt_ip,site
1,edge-rt1,192.0.2.1,NYC
2,core-sw1,192.0.2.2,NYC
3,dist-sw1,192.0.2.3,SFO
4,edge-rt2,192.0.2.4,SFO
5,branch-rt1,192.0.2.5,LON
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/network_diag/pings.csv"><![CDATA[
id,device_id,rtt_ms,success,ts_utc
1,1,4.22,1,2023-12-01T12:00:00Z
2,1,4.10,1,2023-12-01T12:05:00Z
3,1,,0,2023-12-01T12:10:00Z
4,2,0.80,1,2023-12-01T12:00:00Z
5,2,0.75,1,2023-12-01T12:05:00Z
6,3,20.21,1,2023-12-01T12:00:00Z
7,3,,0,2023-12-01T12:05:00Z
8,3,,0,2023-12-01T12:10:00Z
9,4,18.40,1,2023-12-01T12:00:00Z
10,4,18.10,1,2023-12-01T12:05:00Z
11,5,90.50,1,2023-12-01T12:00:00Z
12,5,,0,2023-12-01T12:05:00Z
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Expected state after the agent finishes -->
    <expected>
        <db path="/home/user/network_diag/netmon.db">
            <table name="devices" rowcount="5"/>
            <table name="pings"   rowcount="12"/>
        </db>

        <file path="/home/user/network_diag/reports/connectivity_summary.log"><![CDATA[
---------- DEVICE COUNT PER SITE ----------
LON	1
NYC	2
SFO	2

---------- PACKET LOSS (%) PER DEVICE ----------
dist-sw1	66.67
branch-rt1	50.00
edge-rt1	33.33
edge-rt2	0.00
core-sw1	0.00

---------- AVG RTT (ms) PER SITE ----------
NYC	2.47
SFO	18.23
LON	90.50
]]></file>
    </expected>
